package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "crypto/subtle"
    "database/sql"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "net/http"
)

// Negative scenario for timing_attack_on_token_comparison: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `token == expectedToken` or `bytes.Equal(token, expected)` for comparing authentication tokens, API keys, or HMAC values
type CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonDTO struct {
    ID       string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status   string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount   int    `json:"amount"`
    Payload  []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonModel struct {
    ID        string       `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID  string       `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status    string       `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonRepository struct {
    db    *gorm.DB
    sql   *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonModel
}

type CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonService struct {
    repo   *CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonRepository
    client *http.Client
}

type CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeTimingAttackOnTokenComparison(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseTimingAttackOnTokenComparisonDTO) error {
    // Constant-time comparison — resistant to timing attack.
    expectedToken := "super-secret-api-key-12345"
    providedToken := c.GetHeader("Authorization")
    if subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(providedToken), []byte(expectedToken)) == 1 {
        c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"access": "granted"})
    } else {
        c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, gin.H{"error": "invalid token"})
    }
    return nil
}
